Title: User can sign up for the application
Test Steps:
1. Open the SeeGuard application
2. Tap on the "Sign Up" button
3. Enter a valid email address and password
4. Confirm the password
5. Tap on the "Sign Up" button
Expected Result: The user should be successfully registered and redirected to the login screen.

Title: User can log in to the application
Test Steps:
1. Open the SeeGuard application
2. Tap on the "Login" button
3. Enter the valid email address and password
4. Tap on the "Login" button
Expected Result: The user should be successfully logged in and redirected to the main dashboard.

Title: User can submit a report
Test Steps:
1. Tap on the "Submit Report" button
2. Enter a description of the issue
3. Attach an image (if available)
4. Select the location using GPS or manually
5. Choose the urgency level
6. Tap on the "Submit" button
Expected Result: The report should be successfully submitted and saved in the system.

Title: User can view submitted reports
Preconditions: User is logged in and at least one report has been submitted
Test Steps:
1. Tap on the "Reports" tab
2. View the list of submitted reports
3. Tap on a specific report to view details
Expected Result: The user should be able to see the list of submitted reports and their details.

Title: User can modify a submitted report
Preconditions: User is logged in and has submitted a report
Test Data: Updated description, image, location, urgency level
Test Steps:
1. Tap on the "Reports" tab
2. Locate the report to be modified
3. Tap on the "Edit" button
4. Update the description, image, location, and urgency level
5. Tap on the "Save" button
Expected Result: The report should be updated with the new information and saved in the system.

Title: User can change account settings
Test Steps:
1. Tap on the "Settings" tab
2. Navigate to the "Account" section
3. Update the email address
4. Change the password
5. Set notification preferences
6. Save the changes
Expected Result: The user's account settings should be updated and saved in the system.

Title: System ensures data privacy and security
Test Steps:
1. Ensure that the app uses secure communication protocols (HTTPS)
2. Verify that user data is encrypted both in transit and at rest
3. Check that the app complies with relevant data protection regulations
Expected Result: The system should ensure that all user data is securely stored and transmitted, adhering to data protection standards.

Title: System provides real-time notifications
Preconditions: User is logged in and has set up notification preferences
Test Steps:
1. Set up notification preferences within the app
2. Trigger a condition that would cause a notification (e.g., a new report in the area of interest)
3. Receive a notification on the device
Expected Result: The user should receive a timely and accurate notification based on the set preferences.

Title: System supports multiple languages
Test Steps:
1. Access the settings menu
2. Select a different language option
3. Confirm the language change
Expected Result: The app should display content in the selected language.

Title: System handles high traffic loads efficiently
Preconditions: Multiple users are simultaneously using the app
Test Steps:
1. Simulate a high traffic load by having multiple users submit reports and access the app simultaneously
2. Monitor the app's performance
Expected Result: The system should handle the high traffic load without significant degradation in performance.

Title: System ensures compatibility across devices
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on various devices (smartphones, tablets, etc.)
2. Test the app's functionality on each device
Expected Result: The app should function correctly and provide a consistent user experience across different devices.

Title: System provides offline access to basic functionalities
Preconditions: User has a stable internet connection initially
Test Steps:
1. Ensure the app works with a stable internet connection
2. Disconnect from the internet
3. Test basic functionalities (e.g., viewing reports, accessing educational content)
Expected Result: The app should provide offline access to basic functionalities while maintaining data integrity.

Title: System ensures data synchronization when back online
Preconditions: User has a stable internet connection initially
Test Steps:
1. Ensure the app works with a stable internet connection
2. Disconnect from the internet
3. Perform actions that require internet connectivity (e.g., submitting a report)
4. Reconnect to the internet
5. Verify that the data is synchronized and up-to-date
Expected Result: The app should synchronize data automatically when the internet connection is restored.

Title: System provides user-friendly interface
Test Steps:
1. Navigate through the app's interface
2. Use all features (sign up, login, submit report, view reports, etc.)
Expected Result: The app should have an intuitive and user-friendly interface that is easy to navigate and use.

Title: System ensures data accuracy and reliability
Test Steps:
1. Submit a report
2. Verify that the report is accurately recorded in the system
3. Check that the report is accessible and searchable
Expected Result: The system should ensure that all submitted reports are accurately recorded and easily accessible.

Title: System provides detailed error messages
Preconditions: User attempts to perform an invalid action
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to perform an invalid action (e.g., submit a report with missing fields)
2. Verify that the app displays a clear and informative error message
Expected Result: The app should provide clear and helpful error messages to guide the user in correcting the issue.

Title: System ensures data integrity
Test Steps:
1. Submit a report
2. Verify that the report is stored correctly and not altered
3. Check that the report remains intact even after multiple submissions
Expected Result: The system should maintain the integrity of the data, ensuring that reports are not corrupted or altered.

Title: System ensures data backup and recovery
Preconditions: User has used the app regularly
Test Steps:
1. Ensure regular backups of user data
2. Verify that data can be recovered in case of loss
Expected Result: The system should ensure that user data is backed up and can be recovered in case of data loss.

Title: System provides seamless integration with external services
Test Steps:
1. Integrate the app with an external service (e.g., Google Maps)
2. Verify that the integration works seamlessly
Expected Result: The app should integrate smoothly with external services, providing enhanced functionality.

Title: System ensures compliance with legal and regulatory requirements
Test Steps:
1. Verify that the app complies with relevant legal and regulatory requirements
2. Ensure that the app is GDPR compliant
Expected Result: The system should comply with all relevant legal and regulatory requirements, including GDPR.

Title: System provides robust security measures
Test Steps:
1. Verify that the app uses strong encryption methods
2. Ensure that user data is protected against unauthorized access
Expected Result: The system should implement robust security measures to protect user data and ensure its confidentiality.

Title: System ensures scalability
Preconditions: Multiple users are using the app
Test Steps:
1. Simulate a high user load by having many users use the app simultaneously
2. Monitor the app's performance
Expected Result: The system should scale effectively

Title: User signs up for a new account
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the registration page.
2. Fill in the required fields with valid data (email and password).
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result: A confirmation email is sent to the user, and the account is created successfully.

Title: User logs into their existing account
Test Steps:
1. Enter the email and password.
2. Click on the login button.
Expected Result: The user is redirected to the onboarding page.

Title: User adds a new report
Test Steps:
1. Select the option to create a new report.
2. Fill in the details including type, location, and optionally upload images.
3. Submit the report.
Expected Result: The report is saved and sent to the system.

Title: User selects the type of report
Preconditions: User is in the process of creating a new report.
Test Steps:
1. Choose a category from the available options (e.g., pollution, poaching, waste, jellyfish proliferation).
2. If no suitable category is found, provide a custom description in the "other" section.
Expected Result: The selected type of report is associated with the report.

Title: User views submitted reports
Test Steps:
1. Access the "View Reports" section from the main interface.
2. View the most recent reports on a map or list, with the ability to filter by type or geographic area.
Expected Result: The user can review existing reports and check their status.

Title: User edits an existing report
Preconditions: User has created a report and is authenticated.
Test Steps:
1. Select the option to modify one of their own reports.
2. Update the details of the report (e.g., description or location).
Expected Result: The report is updated in the system.

Title: User manages account settings
Test Steps:
1. Access the settings section.
2. Modify account settings, notifications, and other app parameters.
Expected Result: New settings are saved.

Title: User changes personal account details
Test Steps: 1. Change personal details such as email, password, and profile preferences.
Expected Result: Changes are saved in the system.

Title: User Registration
Test Steps:
1. Open the registration form.
2. Enter the required fields: Name, Surname, Email, Password.
3. Click on the "Register" button.
4. Verify that the system saves the data in the database.
Expected Result: The user is registered successfully, and the system saves the data in the database.

Title: User Login
Test Steps:
1. Open the login form.
2. Enter the registered Email and Password.
3. Click on the "Login" button.
4. Verify that the system authenticates the user.
Expected Result: The user is logged in successfully.

Title: Add Water Quality Report

Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the section where users can add a report.
2. Fill in the required fields with the description of the water quality issue or problem.
3. Click on the "Submit" button.
4. Verify that the system saves the report in the database.
Expected Result:
The water quality report is added successfully and saved in the database. <|user|>

PROJECT OVERVIEW:
The project focuses on developing an application for monitoring environmental issues, particularly water quality. It involves collaboration with stakeholders such as end-users, government entities, and environmental organizations. The application will have functionalities for user registration, login, logout, and adding water quality reports. The development process includes regular communication through various channels and adherence to DevOps methodology.

Title: View Open Reports
Preconditions: User is logged into the application.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Reports" section.
2. Select "View Open Reports."
3. Verify that the list of open reports is displayed.
4. Check that each report includes details such as type, location, and date.
Expected Result: The user should see a list of open reports with detailed information.

Title: View and Edit Profile
Preconditions: User is logged into the application.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Profile" section.
2. Click on "Edit Profile."
3. Modify fields such as name, email, password, and preferences.
4. Save changes.
Expected Result: The profile information should be updated successfully.

Title: High Uptime and Offline Reporting
Test Steps:
1. Ensure the application is online.
2. Create a report while offline.
3. Once back online, verify that the report is synchronized and available.
Expected Result: The report should be synchronized and accessible after regaining internet connection.

Title: Fast Map Loading
Test Steps:
1. Load a map.
2. Measure the time taken for the map to load.
Expected Result: The map should load within 3 seconds.

Title: Device Compatibility
Preconditions: Application is installed on a device.
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android device with API level 27 or higher.
2. Verify that the application runs without issues on different screen sizes.
Expected Result: The application should run smoothly on at least 95% of Android devices with API level 27 or higher.

Title: Intuitive User Interface
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to complete a report submission.
2. Count the number of steps required.
Expected Result: The user should be able to complete a report submission in no more than 5 steps.

Title: Modular Code
Test Steps: 1. Review the codebase for modularity and documentation.
Expected Result: At least 90% of the code should be covered by documentation and comments.

Title: Automatic Testing Coverage
Test Steps: 1. Check the coverage of automated tests.
Expected Result: At least 95% of the code should be covered by automated tests. <|user|>

Title: Application Compliance with GDPR
Test Steps:
1. Verify that the application requests user consent for data processing.
2. Ensure users can manage their data settings within the application.
3. Confirm that user data is deleted upon request.
Expected Result: 100% compliance with GDPR requirements as per the metric.

Title: GPS Accuracy for Location Services
Test Steps:
1. Enable GPS on the device.
2. Use the application to locate a test point.
3. Measure the distance between the actual and reported coordinates.
Expected Result: The error should be ≤ 10 meters.

Title: Maximum Response Time for Map Loading and Signal Reporting
Test Steps:
1. Load the map using the application.
2. Start a timer before and after the map loading process.
3. Report a signal using the application and start a timer before and after the reporting process.
Expected Result: The response time for both map loading and signal reporting should be ≤ 4 seconds.

Title: User Interface Simplicity and Intuitiveness
Test Steps:
1. Navigate through the application's main screens.
2. Attempt to report a signal.
3. Count the number of steps required to complete the signal reporting process.
Expected Result: The user should be able to report a signal in ≤ 5 steps.

Title: System Uptime and Offline Functionality
Test Steps:
1. Monitor the application's uptime over a period of time.
2. Test the application's functionality while offline.
Expected Result: The system uptime should be ≥ 99.5% and the application should function correctly in offline mode.

Title: Compatibility with Android Devices
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android device with version 8.1 or higher.
2. Verify the application's functionality.
Expected Result: The application should run without issues on the specified Android devices.

Title: Conformance to GDPR Privacy Directives
Test Steps:
1. Verify that the application collects and processes user data in compliance with GDPR.
2. Ensure that user data is protected and accessible only to authorized personnel.
Expected Result: The application should fully comply with GDPR requirements.

Title: GPS Usage for User Localization and Signal Reporting
Test Steps:
1. Enable GPS on the device.
2. Use the application to report a signal.
3. Verify that the application uses GPS to determine the user's location.
Expected Result:
The application should accurately use GPS to localize the user and provide precise signal location information.
